problem: The marketing team wanted animated particle and parallax backgrounds on hero sections across all breakpoints. On mobile this harms LCP, drains battery, and conflicts with reduced-motion preferences.
context: The marketing-saas-genome permits expressive motion but its component_conventions state 'particle and parallax effects disabled on mobile and reduced-motion', with an LCP target under 2.5s and a 150KB above-the-fold JS budget.
alternatives: Particles everywhere including mobile — maximal visual impact, worst performance and accessibility
Static gradient on mobile, particles on desktop only, all gated by prefers-reduced-motion
Remove particles entirely on all breakpoints
decision: Disable particle and parallax effects on mobile breakpoints and whenever prefers-reduced-motion is set; desktop may use them only via a dynamically imported, below-the-fold animation bundle.
rationale: Mobile field data showed particle scripts pushing LCP past budget on mid-tier devices. Restricting them protects conversion-critical load time while preserving desktop expressiveness, consistent with the genome.
evidence: evidence/lcp-field-data-marketing.json: particle hero raised mobile LCP by ~1.4s on mid-tier devices
accessibility_impact: Honours prefers-reduced-motion across all breakpoints; eliminates large background motion that can trigger vestibular discomfort.
performance_impact: Keeps mobile LCP under 2.5s and the animation library out of the above-the-fold bundle via dynamic import.
implementation: Particle component guarded by a viewport and reduced-motion check; desktop bundle dynamically imported below the fold. Exception: campaign-microsite-particles (expires 2026-09-30).
validation: Validated against marketing LCP field data and the Q2 conversion A/B test.
